commands.review_summary:
The PR routes unresumable WebChat restart-recovery notices into the session transcript and adds a regression test instead of sending WebChat through outbound `message.action`.

PR surface: Source +42, Tests +36. Total +78 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main rejects WebChat as a deliverable `message.action` channel, so unresumable WebChat restart notices need a transcript path instead. I did not run a live restart because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed because the branch has blocking code findings and the external proof is only a Vitest/on-disk fixture rather than real WebChat restart proof.

target.title: fix(agents): show WebChat the restart recovery notice via the session transcript
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87808.

When the Gateway restarts mid-turn during a WebChat direct chat (bundled Codex harness), the interrupted turn is marked unresumable and the user is supposed to receive a recovery notice. But that notice is delivered via `message.action` (an outbound channel send), and WebChat is an internal-only channel that rejects `message.action`. So `resolveRestartRecoveryDeliveryContext` returns `undefined` (WebChat fails `isDeliverableMessageChannel`), the notice is silently dropped, and on reconnect the turn just vanishes â€” no assistant reply, no interruption notice. The user has to send another message to discover what happened.

## What changed

**`src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts`** â€” in `sendUnresumableSessionNotice`, detect the WebChat (internal) channel from the raw delivery channel *before* the deliverable-channel filter. For WebChat, persist the recovery notice to the session transcript via `appendInjectedAssistantMessageToTranscript` (a gateway-injected assistant message written to disk + a live transcript update) instead of an outbound `message.action`, so a reconnecting WebChat client renders the terminal notice. Non-WebChat channels keep the existing `message.action` delivery.

**`src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`** â€” regression test: an unresumable WebChat session no longer issues an outbound `message.action`, and the recovery notice is persisted to the transcript (read back from disk).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** An interrupted WebChat turn now leaves a visible terminal notice in the session transcript instead of silently vanishing on reconnect. The notice is persisted to disk so a reconnecting WebChat client renders it; non-WebChat channels keep their outbound delivery.

**Real environment tested:** Local worktree of `origin/main` at base `118060157d`, Node v22.22.0. Drove the real `recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions` â†’ `sendUnresumableSessionNotice` path via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`, against a real on-disk session store + transcript (`sessions.json` + `main-session.jsonl`). The notice is read back from the real transcript file.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts -t "WebChat transcript"
# Baseline measured by git-stashing the recovery fix and re-running the same test.
```

**Evidence after fix:** With the fix stashed (origin behavior), the WebChat session's transcript never gets the notice and the test fails:
```
src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts (21 tests | 1 failed | 20 skipped)
AssertionError: expected '{"message":{"role":"user",...}}' to contain "couldn't safely resume"
```
With the fix applied, the notice is persisted and the full suite passes:
```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  21 passed (21)
```

**Observed result after fix:** The unresumable WebChat session no longer issues an outbound `message.action` (WebChat would reject it); instead the recovery notice ("â€¦couldn't safely resumeâ€¦") is written to the session transcript on disk, where a reconnecting WebChat client reads it. Session status is still marked `failed`.

**What was not tested:** No live Gateway-restart end-to-end run against a real WebChat client; the change is in the deterministic restart-recovery delivery path, exercised against a real on-disk session store + transcript. The fix follows the reviewer's pointer (the `main-session-restart-recovery.ts` notice path vs `send.ts` WebChat `message.action` rejection): WebChat notices now go through transcript persistence.

## Validation (supplemental)

- `main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts` full suite â†’ 21 passed (before fix: WebChat case fails as shown above)
- `tsgo:core:test` â†’ no new errors in changed files; `oxlint` â†’ clean
